# kCTParagraphStyleAttributeName

**Global Variable**

The paragraph style of the text to which this attribute applies.

## Declaration

```swift
let kCTParagraphStyleAttributeName: CFString
```

## Discussion

A paragraph style object is used to specify things like line alignment, tab rulers, writing direction, and so on. Value must be a [doc://com.apple.coretext/documentation/CoreText/CTParagraphStyle] object. Default is an empty [doc://com.apple.coretext/documentation/CoreText/CTParagraphStyle] object.

## Related Documentation

- **CTParagraphStyle**: Paragraph or ruler attributes in an attributed string.

## Constants

- **kCTCharacterShapeAttributeName**: Controls glyph selection.
- **kCTFontAttributeName**: The font of the text to which this attribute applies.
- **kCTKernAttributeName**: The amount to kern the next character.
- **kCTLigatureAttributeName**: The type of ligatures to use.
- **kCTForegroundColorAttributeName**: The foreground color of the text to which this attribute applies.
- **kCTForegroundColorFromContextAttributeName**: Sets a foreground color using the context’s fill color.
- **kCTStrokeWidthAttributeName**: The stroke width.
- **kCTStrokeColorAttributeName**: The stroke color.
- **kCTSuperscriptAttributeName**: Controls vertical text positioning.
- **kCTUnderlineColorAttributeName**: The underline color.
- **kCTUnderlineStyleAttributeName**: The style of underlining, to be applied at render time, for the text to which this attribute applies.
- **kCTVerticalFormsAttributeName**: The orientation of the glyphs in the text to which this attribute applies.
- **kCTGlyphInfoAttributeName**: The glyph info object to apply to the text associated with this attribute.
- **kCTRunDelegateAttributeName**: The run-delegate object to apply to an attribute range of the string.
- **kCTBaselineOffsetAttributeName**: Vertical offset for text position.
